category_title: Adolescents positioned as struggling readers who disengage from standardized tasks but engage with self-selected literature
minimal_context: The student and tutor are in a bookstore looking at the Babysitter Club book series to select books for independent reading.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the Babysitter Club book series. You better read them.

STUDENT: I've never really been interested in those books. They just looked boring to me.

TUTOR: Just pick out some books to start reading.

STUDENT: You're really going to get me these books? I'm probably not even going to read them.

TUTOR: Read the back cover and see what it says.

STUDENT: (Reads the back cover) This looks like this is not my type and I really don't care for this one.

options.A: Nod and try to maintain eye contact with the student for as long as possible.
options.B: Grant full agency by telling the student to pick out any books she would probably like.
options.C: Accurately model the correct pronunciation of the words on the back cover of the book.
options.D: Provide opportunities to respond as a structured form of tutor attention during the selection process.
